# Axion Mobile 1.0.2

**Full-playlist import is here.**

Pasting a YouTube / YT Music playlist URL now queues every track in the
playlist, not just the first one. Drops in as an update over v1.0.1 —
signing key, package id and database schema are unchanged, so your
library and settings are preserved.

---

## What's new

### Playlist URLs expand to every track

**Before (v1.0.1):** pasting `https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL...`
imported only the first track — the message even said so in the Jobs
panel.

**Now (v1.0.2):** the importer fans the playlist out into one task per
track. The Jobs panel shows every track, each prefixed with the
playlist name so they group visually:

```
[Discovery] Daft Punk — One More Time
[Discovery] Daft Punk — Aerodynamic
[Discovery] Daft Punk — Digital Love
...
```

Resolution happens via Piped's `/playlists/{id}` endpoint with
`/nextpage` pagination, falling back to Invidious's
`/api/v1/playlists/{id}?page=N&page_size=200` when Piped is down or
rate-limited. Same two-backend story as single-track imports.

### Concurrency limiter (max 3 parallel downloads)

A long playlist used to translate into N simultaneous requests against
the same Piped instance, which triggered rate limits and 429s after
~5 tracks. v1.0.2 ships a simple semaphore in the import pipeline:

- **≤ 3 downloads running at any time**
- The rest sit in `queued` status, picked up as slots free
- Applies to every source (YouTube, SoundCloud, direct), not just
  playlists — single-track bursts benefit too

You can still **cancel individual tracks** from the Jobs panel; queued
ones simply never get a slot.

### Playlist size cap

Playlists are capped at **500 tracks** per paste. This is a safety
limit: YT-Music playlists can be effectively infinite (e.g. "Top 5000
songs"), and we'd rather truncate than silently queue a 4 GB download.
If you hit the cap you'll see a warning in the app log; paste the
playlist again from a later point to continue.

## Downloads

| File | Size | Recommended for |
|---|---|---|
| `Axion-1.0.2-arm64-v8a.apk` | ~31 MB | **Most users.** All modern 64-bit Android phones. |
| `Axion-1.0.2-armeabi-v7a.apk` | ~25 MB | Older / cheaper 32-bit devices (Android 8–10 era). |
| `Axion-1.0.2-x86_64.apk` | ~33 MB | Emulators and Chromebooks. |
| `Axion-1.0.2-universal.apk` | ~83 MB | "I don't know which one" — works on every architecture. |

## Installation

Just open the new APK on top of v1.0.0 / v1.0.1 — library and settings
are preserved. First-time installers: see the
[v1.0.0 install guide](https://github.com/almostkoi/axion-mobile/releases/tag/v1.0.0).

## Verifying the APK

```powershell
Get-FileHash .\Axion-1.0.2-arm64-v8a.apk -Algorithm SHA256
```

Same signing certificate as v1.0.0 / v1.0.1:

```
Owner:   CN=almostkoi, OU=Axion, O=Axion
Issuer:  same (self-signed)
Valid:   until April 2056
```

## Full changelog

- **feat(yt):** pasting a playlist URL now imports every track (up to 500) via Piped `/playlists` + `/nextpage` pagination, with Invidious fallback
- **feat(import):** added global semaphore (max 3 concurrent downloads) so long playlists don't trip instance rate limits
- **refactor(import):** extracted single-track enqueue helper; `startImport` now dispatches between single-track and playlist fan-out
- **chore(yt):** removed first-track-only playlist logic from `importFromYouTube` — playlists are fully expanded at dispatch time, so the inline fallback is no longer reachable
- **chore(version):** bump to 1.0.2 (`versionCode` 10002)

## Known limitations

- Playlist imports run sequentially in groups of 3. A 50-track playlist at
  ~5 MB / track takes roughly 5–8 minutes on a 10 Mbps connection, or a
  minute on a decent Wi-Fi link. Patience.
- No resume across app restarts: closing Axion mid-playlist cancels the
  queued tracks. Already-downloaded tracks are kept.
- Private / members-only playlists don't work (Piped and Invidious
  won't see them without auth).

## Reporting issues

Open an issue with:

- Axion Mobile version (Settings → About)
- Phone model + Android version
- Steps to reproduce
- Logs via `adb logcat -s AxionMobile:V ReactNativeJS:V *:S` if you can capture them
